holdings.infoBox: AMOM is an equity ETF with a total of 51 individual holdings. The top holdings are Micron Technology stock at 5.26%, Cisco Systems at 4.87%, Caterpillar at 4.68%, Advanced Micro Devices at 4.34%, and Intel at 3.66%.

overview.description: The QRAFT AI-Enhanced U.S. Large Cap Momentum ETF (AMOM) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in large cap equity. The fund is an actively-managed portfolio of U.S. Large Cap equities chosen with the aid of artificial intelligence. AMOM was launched on May 21, 2019 and is issued by Exchange Traded Concepts.

data.title: AI Trader Outperformed in 2024. Nvidia, Tesla, and Broadcom Are Some of Its Favorites.
data.text: SoftBank-backed Qraft Technologies has three AI-powered exchange-traded funds, including its momentum fund AMOM.

data.title: Broad A.I. Play: AMOM ETF
data.text: Qraft's Francis Oh joins to talk about the AMOM A.I. ETF. Top holdings include Nvidia (NVDA), Apple (AAPL), and Eli Lilly (LLY).

data.title: Qraft Technologies' Flagship ETFs AMOM and QRFT Celebrate 5th Anniversary with Powerful Performance Milestones
data.text: NEW YORK & SEOUL, South Korea--(BUSINESS WIRE)---- $AMOM #AI--Two of the earliest AI-driven ETFs, AMOM and QRFT, celebrate their 5-year anniversary and successful track record of strong performance.

data.text: SEOUL, South Korea--(BUSINESS WIRE)---- $AMOM--Qraft Technologies welcomes Robert Nestor, former Blackrock and Vanguard executive, to lead growth initiatives for artificial intelligence firm.

data.text: SEOUL, South Korea--(BUSINESS WIRE)---- $AMOM #AI--Qraft AI announces its AMOM ETF has surpassed $50 million in assets under management. The fund's success is backed by an active, AI-driven approach.

data.title: This robot-run fund loaded up on Tesla because it thinks the stock will soar. It's been right before.
data.text: An exchange-traded fund driven by artificial intelligence has just loaded up on shares in Tesla — and it has a history of correctly predicting the stock's price swings.

data.title: Qraft AI ETFs Post Strong Third Quarter Returns
